nodes.name: InitializeHMCKernelState
nodes.description: Construct immutable HMC kernel/state specification from target log-density, initial latent position, and integrator hyperparameters. Includes explicit latent state, cached log-probability/gradient slots, and mass-matrix assumptions.

outputs.name: chain_state_0
outputs.constraints: contains position, logp_current, gradient, momenta placeholder, trace init

nodes.type_signature: (target: callable oracle, initial_positions: vector/tensor, step_size: float, n_leapfrog: int) -> tuple[StateModelSpec, HMCState]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Immutable Hamiltonian Transition State Initializer. Builds a fixed transition specification and an initial latent-state record for a deterministic, gradient-informed dynamical sampler. The transform maps a differentiable scalar field oracle, an initial coordinate vector, and integration hyperparameters into a normalized state object with explicit coordinates, deferred momentum storage, and cache slots for scalar-field value and first derivative. It also materializes metric assumptions as immutable metadata so downstream updates use a consistent geometry.. Applications: Initializing trajectory-based inference for inverse-parameter estimation in systems biology ODE/PDE models, Bootstrapping constrained latent-state exploration in robot dynamics calibration with differentiable simulators, Setting up gradient-driven posterior traversal for geophysical tomography parameter fields, Preparing high-dimensional probabilistic state evolution in computational materials microstructure reconstruction

nodes.name: InitializeSamplerRNG
nodes.description: Initialize explicit stochastic state for pure functional sampling. RNG state is threaded across all transitions and never mutated in place.

outputs.name: prng_key_state
outputs.constraints: immutable key to be split per transition

nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic Stochastic-State Bootstrap. Maps a single integer entropy token into an immutable pseudo-random state object that can drive probabilistic transitions in a purely functional pipeline. The produced state is not mutated; instead, successor states are derived and explicitly threaded through subsequent computations. This creates reproducible stochastic behavior where identical seeds yield identical random-state trajectories.. Applications: Monte Carlo trajectory initialization in computational physics with reproducible experiment replay, Deterministic randomized augmentation pipelines in machine learning training workflows, Reproducible randomized search initialization in combinatorial optimization and metaheuristics, Procedural pattern/structure generation in graphics or digital fabrication where exact regeneration is required

nodes.name: HamiltonianTransitionKernel
nodes.description: Perform one pure HMC transition: generate/consume momenta, run leapfrog integrator proposal, evaluate acceptance, and return a brand-new chain state plus updated RNG key.

inputs.name: prng_key_in
inputs.constraints: must be explicitly provided and split

outputs.name: transition_stats
outputs.constraints: contains accept/reject info and energy diagnostics

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: HMCState, kernel_spec: StateModelSpec, prng_key_in: PRNGKey/RNG state, logp_oracle: callable) -> tuple[HMCState, PRNGKey/RNG state, dict]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Energy-Conserving Augmented-State Transition Operator. The operator maps a current point in a differentiable scalar field to a candidate point by augmenting the state with Gaussian auxiliary variables, then simulating a finite sequence of symplectic updates on the joint system. It computes a scalar energy discrepancy between pre- and post-simulation states and applies a stochastic accept/reject correction to preserve the target invariant distribution despite discretization error. The result is an immutable next state plus diagnostics and an advanced random stream state.. Applications: Calibrating latent parameters in climate or fluid simulation models where gradients are available through differentiable solvers, Posterior sampling of high-dimensional neural network parameters in Bayesian deep learning, Inferring source parameters in inverse problems for medical imaging reconstruction, Estimating hidden biophysical parameters in systems biology differential-equation models

nodes.name: infer_chain_geometry
nodes.description: Read the position tensor shape to determine batch size and latent dimension for this transition.

inputs.name: state_in.positions
inputs.constraints: Rank-2; first axis is chain axis.

nodes.name: sample_transition_randomness
nodes.description: Consume RNG state to draw initial Gaussian momenta and per-chain uniform variates for Metropolis acceptance.
nodes.type_signature: (prng_key_in: PRNGKey, n_chains: usize, dim: usize) -> tuple[Tensor[n_chains, dim], Tensor[n_chains], PRNGKey]

nodes.name: evaluate_current_log_density_and_initial_half_grad
nodes.description: Evaluate current log density and compute the initial half-step momentum increment from the position gradient.

outputs.name: logp_current
outputs.constraints: Unnormalized log density at current positions.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in.positions: Tensor[n_chains, dim], kernel_spec.step_size: scalar, logp_oracle: callable(position_batch) -> Tensor[n_chains]) -> tuple[Tensor[n_chains], Tensor[n_chains, dim]]

nodes.name: compute_current_hamiltonian
nodes.description: Compute current kinetic energy and Hamiltonian from sampled momenta and current log density.

nodes.name: integrate_leapfrog_proposal
nodes.description: Run n_leapfrog symplectic updates to produce proposed positions/momenta and final proposed log density.

outputs.name: logp_proposed
outputs.constraints: Unnormalized log density at proposed positions.

outputs.name: grad_half_last
outputs.constraints: Final half-step gradient increment from last leapfrog substep.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in.positions: Tensor[n_chains, dim], momentum_0: Tensor[n_chains, dim], grad_half_0: Tensor[n_chains, dim], kernel_spec.step_size: scalar, kernel_spec.n_leapfrog: usize, logp_oracle: callable(position_batch) -> Tensor[n_chains]) -> tuple[Tensor[n_chains, dim], Tensor[n_chains, dim], Tensor[n_chains], Tensor[n_chains, dim]]

nodes.name: compute_acceptance_log_ratio
nodes.description: Compute proposed Hamiltonian and per-chain Metropolis log acceptance ratio.
nodes.type_signature: (h_current: Tensor[n_chains], proposed_momenta: Tensor[n_chains, dim], logp_proposed: Tensor[n_chains]) -> tuple[Tensor[n_chains], Tensor[n_chains]]

nodes.name: derive_acceptance_mask
nodes.description: Convert uniform draws and log acceptance ratio into a boolean accept/reject decision per chain.

nodes.name: assemble_transition_result
nodes.description: Construct next chain state by selecting proposed vs current positions per chain and package diagnostics.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: Positions are masked-select(current, proposed); includes updated cached gradient if state tracks it.

outputs.name: prng_key_out_final
outputs.constraints: Forwarded unchanged from RNG sampling output.

outputs.name: transition_stats
outputs.constraints: Contains per-chain acceptance, energies, and acceptance log-ratios.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: HMCState, proposed_positions: Tensor[n_chains, dim], accept_mask: Tensor[n_chains] (bool), grad_half_last: Tensor[n_chains, dim], prng_key_out: PRNGKey, h_current: Tensor[n_chains], h_proposed: Tensor[n_chains], accept_log_ratio: Tensor[n_chains]) -> tuple[HMCState, PRNGKey, dict]

nodes.name: CollectPosteriorChain
nodes.description: Drive warmup/discard and collection loops by repeatedly applying the transition kernel; optionally emit progress while preserving pure state threading.

inputs.name: prng_key_state
inputs.constraints: explicit RNG flow through all iterations

inputs.name: logp_oracle
inputs.constraints: finite log-density evaluator used by each transition

nodes.type_signature: (n_collect: int, n_discard: int, chain_state_0: HMCState, kernel_spec: StateModelSpec, prng_key_state: PRNGKey/RNG state, logp_oracle: Callable[[NDArray[np.float64]], float]) -> tuple[array, HMCState, PRNGKey/RNG state, array/dict]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Two-Phase Markov State Rollout and Retained Trajectory Extraction. Applies a stochastic state-transition operator repeatedly to an immutable state object while explicitly threading random-generator state through every step. The iteration is split into a non-retained prefix phase (discard/warmup) and a retained phase (collection), producing an ordered subset of visited states plus diagnostic metadata. Optional progress emission is side-effecting observability that does not alter the pure input-to-output state evolution.. Applications: Statistical physics: generate equilibrated microstate trajectories after thermalization for lattice/spin or particle simulations, Computational biology: sample high-dimensional conformational states of macromolecular systems with initial equilibration removed, Robotics and control: propagate belief-state particles through a stochastic dynamics model with burn-in before logging planning samples, Reliability engineering: simulate long-run system-state evolution under random shocks, discarding transient startup behavior before analysis

nodes.name: InitializeCollectionContext
nodes.description: Derive chain dimensions from the initial state, allocate the time-major sample buffer, and initialize trace/progress bookkeeping state.
nodes.type_signature: (n_collect: int, chain_state_0: HMCState, kernel_spec: StateModelSpec) -> tuple[array/tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim], tuple(int n_chains, int dim), chain trace accumulator]

nodes.name: RunDiscardPhase
nodes.description: Apply the transition kernel for burn-in/discard steps while threading chain state and RNG state.
nodes.type_signature: (n_discard: int, chain_state_0: HMCState, kernel_spec: StateModelSpec, prng_key_state: PRNGKey/RNG state) -> tuple[HMCState, PRNGKey/RNG state, array/dict]

nodes.name: SeedDiagnosticsFromBurnedState
nodes.description: Register the post-burn-in state as the initial diagnostics sample and update trace state with any recoverable diagnostics warnings.
nodes.type_signature: (chain_state_burned: HMCState, trace_state_0: chain trace accumulator) -> chain trace accumulator

nodes.name: CollectPosteriorTransitions
nodes.description: Iterate n_collect transitions; at each step advance state with kernel, write positions into buffer, and update diagnostics/progress trace.

outputs.name: collection_buffer_filled
outputs.constraints: all collection slots filled in order

nodes.type_signature: (n_collect: int, chain_state_burned: HMCState, prng_key_burned: PRNGKey/RNG state, kernel_spec: StateModelSpec, collection_buffer_0: array/tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim], chain_dims: tuple(int n_chains, int dim), trace_state_seeded: chain trace accumulator) -> tuple[array/tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim], HMCState, PRNGKey/RNG state, chain trace accumulator]

nodes.name: ReorderSampleLayout
nodes.description: Convert collected samples from time-major [collect, chain, dim] layout to chain-major [chain, collect, dim] layout.
nodes.type_signature: (collection_buffer_filled: array/tensor[n_collect, n_chains, dim]) -> array/tensor[n_chains, n_collect, dim]

nodes.name: FinalizeChainTrace
nodes.description: Compute final run diagnostics/statistics from accumulated trace state and samples, producing a pure chain_trace artifact.

outputs.name: chain_trace
outputs.constraints: contains diagnostics summary and optional warnings/progress history

nodes.type_signature: (trace_state_final: chain trace accumulator, discard_events: array/dict, samples: array/tensor[n_chains, n_collect, dim]) -> array/dict
